title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Contract Clinic: Implementation Evidence
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt implementation evidence increment in a coding-agent implementation brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
Request intentional, reviewable changes while preserving unrelated user work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Implementation Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Implementation Evidence contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.

content.text: Use a changed theory case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Implementation Evidence condition decided your prediction.

options.text: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works. This does not resolve predict implementation evidence.

For predict implementation evidence, apply Implementation Evidence without its stated preconditions, omit the a coding-agent implementation brief trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Implementation Evidence for a coding-agent implementation brief.

content.text: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works. This fails the Implementation Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Build Implementation Evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-qx2rbh" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Implementation Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-qx2rbh
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Implementation Evidence structural check is only one gate. a coding-agent implementation brief must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the Implementation Evidence model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Implementation Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed theory case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Implementation Evidence through the concrete a coding-agent implementation brief case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
